fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Jersey Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity new jersey, how to transfer audiologist license to new jersey, audiologist compact new jersey, new jersey audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Advisory Committee
board.parent_dept: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
board.mailing_address:
State Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
P.O. Box 45042
Newark, New Jersey 07101

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in New Jersey.

steps.title: Register and Complete Online Application
steps.description: Visit the MyLicense Online Application System to register as a 'First Time Registrant' or log in. Complete the online application for Audiologist licensure, providing all required personal, educational, and professional information.

steps.title: Arrange for Official Transcripts
steps.description: Have official transcripts, verifying your master's or doctoral degree in Audiology, sent directly from your educational institution to the State Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Submit Official Praxis Exam Scores
steps.description: Ensure your official scores for the Audiology Praxis examination are sent directly from ETS to the New Jersey State Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Background Check (CHBC)
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided on the Board's website to complete a Criminal History Background Check through MorphoTrust/IdentoGO, as required for all applicants.

steps.title: Provide Verification of Clinical Experience (VCE)
steps.description: Submit a completed Verification of Clinical Experience (VCE) form, documenting your supervised post-doctoral experience or Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY), directly to the Board.

steps.title: Pay Application and Licensure Fees
steps.description: Pay the required initial application fee and initial licensure fee through the MyLicense Online Application System. The fees vary based on the application type and the point in the biennial cycle.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Jersey audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in New Jersey. To work in New Jersey, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in New Jersey
hero.dek: Users apply for an Audiologist license in New Jersey through the MyLicense Online Application System, which handles both initial and endorsement applications.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the MyLicense Online Application System and click 'First Time Registrant' to create an account. You will need to provide personal information and set up security questions before proceeding with your application.
